title: Patient Management Service
subtitle: Hospital Billing & Microservices System
description: Architected a hospital billing system as independently deployable microservices using Spring Boot. Enabled asynchronous event streaming via Kafka and low-latency RPC via gRPC. Containerized services using Docker. Implemented authentication and role-based access control using Spring Security.
problem: Hospital billing platforms require high reliability, role-based isolation, and asynchronous coordination to process complex transactions without blocking client operations.
solution: Divided the hospital system domains into separate microservices using Spring Boot, using gRPC for low-latency internal communication and Kafka event streams for asynchronous transaction updates. Containerized each service under Docker and secured endpoints via Spring Security authentication.
architecture: Hospital Gateway -> Spring Security Filter -> Core Microservices (gRPC & REST) -> Kafka Event Streams -> Relational Database Clusters.
challenges: Maintaining database transaction consistency across multiple microservices without double-charging billing channels.
Securing internal gRPC communication channels while keeping request overhead low.
results: Decoupled patient management and billing into independently scalable deployable units.
Reduced system-wide latency by using binary gRPC communication instead of REST.
lessons: Event-driven state synchronization requires careful outbox pattern designs to prevent event duplicates.
Spring Security filter chains must be explicitly configured on gateways to ensure unified CORS and RBAC controls.

title: SAHAI — Mental Health & Lifestyle Platform
subtitle: Smart India Hackathon 2025 National Participant
description: Built a full-stack mental health platform with real-time chat, appointment booking, and community discussion forums. Designed RAG-powered services backed by Pinecone vector database. Built scalable backend services supporting multiple concurrent features.
problem: Accessing reliable, contextual mental wellness resources and therapist appointment systems is often slow, disjointed, and lacks immediate support.
solution: Engineered a wellness platform using Django and FastAPI. Integrated a Pinecone-backed retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) assistant for instant support and configured WebSockets to handle real-time chat rooms between users and therapists.
architecture: React Frontend -> FastAPI (RAG Agent) & Django (Core App) -> Pinecone Vector Store & MySQL Database.
challenges: Handling multiple concurrent chat room WebSocket connections without degrading backend performance.
Structuring prompt templates to prevent the RAG LLM from hallucinating medical claims.
results: Selected as a National Participant in the Smart India Hackathon 2025.
Supported real-time therapist scheduling, community forums, and a sub-second response wellness AI chat.
lessons: WebSockets require resilient connection lifecycle handlers to prevent resource leakage on client disconnections.
Pinecone vector search latency is heavily dependent on embedding chunk sizes; optimized chunking is crucial.

title: ORBITAIR — AI-Powered AQI Forecasting
subtitle: NASA Space Apps Challenge 2025 — Top 5 in India
description: Developed a FastAPI backend with TimescaleDB for geospatial AQI forecasting. Integrated NASA TEMPO, EPA/OpenAQ, and NOAA data feeds. Built a forecasting pipeline achieving 98% prediction accuracy. Created a React and Leaflet dashboard for pollution visualization and explainable AI outputs.
problem: Aggregating geospatial data from NASA TEMPO satellites, NOAA feeds, and EPA sensors in real-time, and forecasting AQI with spatial accuracy, is computationally resource-intensive.
solution: Built a FastAPI time-series backend backed by TimescaleDB to index high-volume geographical points. Created a forecasting pipeline that parses sensor datasets and trains models to predict AQI, rendering outcomes on a Leaflet map.
architecture: FastAPI -> TimescaleDB Time-Series Indexes -> AQI ML Forecasting Engine -> React + Leaflet UI.
challenges: Handling dynamic time-series data ingest pipelines without causing write-locks on the databases.
Mapping complex multi-dimensional geospatial metrics onto a responsive 2D Leaflet canvas.
results: Ranked Top 5 in India in the NASA Space Apps Challenge 2025 (competing against 823 teams).
Achieved a 98% time-series prediction accuracy rate on forecast AQI index points.
lessons: TimescaleDB hypertable partitioning is highly effective for running geospatial queries on large datasets.
Explainable AI outputs are critical for helping city planners trust predictive environmental models.
